classDescription: A master of martial combat and weapon versatility.
roleIdentity: Durable frontline warrior with scalable multi-attack output.

fighter-fighting-style.summary: Lock in a permanent combat specialty that tells the player how this fighter prefers to win exchanges.
fighter-fighting-style.description: Fighting Style is one of the clearest identity levers on the fighter chassis. It should be obvious from the sheet whether the character is built around defense, archery, dueling, great weapons, or another weapon plan, because that decision colors almost every later choice.

fighter-second-wind.summary: Catch your breath and restore a meaningful chunk of staying power without surrendering your whole turn.
fighter-second-wind.description: Second Wind is the fighter’s quick self-stabilizer. It is there so the class can keep operating through chip damage, survive one more exchange, and avoid feeling fragile just because the healer is busy elsewhere.
fighter-second-wind.recovery: Regain all expended uses on a Short or Long Rest.
fighter-second-wind.effect: Immediate self-healing and a steadier front-line presence.

fighter-weapon-mastery.summary: Your weapons do more than deal damage — they carry rider properties that reward choosing the right tool for the job.
fighter-weapon-mastery.description: Weapon Mastery is one of the features that makes the modern fighter feel technical instead of empty. Different weapons now imply different control, pressure, or positioning lines, so the sheet should make those mastered choices visible and easy to understand.

fighter-action-surge-1-use.summary: Explode a turn with an extra action when tempo matters more than patience.
fighter-action-surge-1-use.description: Action Surge is one of the fighter’s signature power spikes. Use it when an extra action changes the whole round: finish a target, reposition and still attack, cast and attack as an Eldritch Knight, or seize the opening before the enemy recovers.
fighter-action-surge-1-use.recovery: Regain all expended uses on a Short or Long Rest.
fighter-action-surge-1-use.effect: Gain one extra action on your turn.

fighter-tactical-mind.summary: Turn disciplined focus into better clutch checks when the group needs someone to solve a problem cleanly.
fighter-tactical-mind.description: Tactical Mind exists so the fighter contributes outside raw attack math. It is a reminder that this class is not only muscle; it is trained battlefield judgment that can turn an important check from failure into success.

fighter-martial-archetype-subclass.summary: Your archetype decides what kind of fighter you really are beyond the base attack engine.
fighter-martial-archetype-subclass.description:
Martial Archetype is the subclass gate for fighter. This is where the class becomes a Battle Master, Champion, Eldritch Knight, or Psi Warrior and starts playing with a distinct tactical identity.

The sheet should treat this as a major fork because later subclass features are what stop high-level fighters from feeling like only “more attacks.”

fighter-indomitable-1-use.summary: Refuse a bad save result when the fight is too important to lose to one failed defense.
fighter-indomitable-1-use.description: Indomitable is the fighter’s answer to swingy saving throws. When a crippling effect would take you out of the scene, this feature gives you another chance to hold the line and keep acting like the martial anchor of the team.
fighter-indomitable-1-use.recovery: Regain all expended uses on a Long Rest.

fighter-tactical-shift.summary: Reposition aggressively without completely giving up your turn economy.
fighter-tactical-shift.description: Tactical Shift makes the fighter feel more active in real maps and not just in a damage spreadsheet. It is about moving to the square that matters while still keeping offensive pressure online.

fighter-studied-attacks.summary: Longer exchanges reward your experience as you read patterns and convert pressure into more reliable offense.
fighter-studied-attacks.description: Studied Attacks is a veteran-fighter feature about momentum. Over repeated rounds, the fighter should feel more certain, more punishing, and harder for enemies to survive through simple repetition.

fighter-ability-score-improvement-4.summary: Increase core stats or pick a feat to sharpen your combat identity early.
fighter-ability-score-improvement-4.description: At this tier the fighter can lock in accuracy, damage, durability, or utility through Ability Score Improvement or a feat. This is a major build pivot that should feel explicit in level-up UI.

fighter-extra-attack-2.summary: Your Attack action now delivers two weapon swings each turn.
fighter-extra-attack-2.description: Extra Attack is the fighter's baseline damage jump at level 5. It defines the class cadence in sustained fights and combines strongly with weapon mastery riders.

fighter-ability-score-improvement-6.summary: Another stat/feat choice keeps your core game plan ahead of encounter scaling.
fighter-ability-score-improvement-6.description: This second early ASI helps fighters stay numerically reliable and lets specialized builds branch into control, defense, or burst options without falling behind.

fighter-subclass-feature-7.summary: Your martial archetype deepens with a defining mid-tier technique.
fighter-subclass-feature-7.description: Level 7 is where subclass identity becomes more than flavor. Surface this as a real tactical unlock tied to your archetype's unique combat approach.

fighter-weapon-mastery-4-weapons.summary: Expand your mastered arsenal to four weapons for better matchup coverage.
fighter-weapon-mastery-4-weapons.description: More mastery slots means broader tactical options: swap between control, push, reach, and damage profiles as encounters change.

fighter-ability-score-improvement-8.summary: Take a third foundational stat/feat step before late-tier threats ramp up.
fighter-ability-score-improvement-8.description: By level 8 this ASI is often used to finish primary ability caps or secure a signature feat that defines the fighter's table role.

fighter-subclass-feature-10.summary: Your archetype grants a stronger advanced pattern at level 10.
fighter-subclass-feature-10.description: This subclass milestone should be presented as a practical new lever in combat planning, not just a passive line item.

fighter-extra-attack-3.summary: Your Attack action now makes three attacks each turn.
fighter-extra-attack-3.description: This major scaling breakpoint keeps the fighter ahead in sustained weapon throughput and reinforces its frontline striker role.

fighter-ability-score-improvement-12.summary: Late-tier ASI/feat selection to refine survivability or burst reliability.
fighter-ability-score-improvement-12.description: At level 12, fighters can either cap key stats or add utility feats that close common tactical weaknesses.

fighter-subclass-feature-13.summary: Subclass kit gains another high-tier specialization feature.
fighter-subclass-feature-13.description: Level 13 subclass features often reinforce the archetype's signature loop; keep their role-specific impact visible in UI text.

fighter-indomitable-2-uses.summary: Gain a second Indomitable use to resist high-stakes failed saves.
fighter-indomitable-2-uses.description: Extra Indomitable uses increase late-game resilience against debilitating control effects that can remove a fighter from a fight.
fighter-indomitable-2-uses.recovery: Regain all expended uses on a Long Rest.

fighter-ability-score-improvement-14.summary: Another ASI/feat window for polishing endgame accuracy and durability.
fighter-ability-score-improvement-14.description: This progression choice helps fighters stay dependable when enemy ACs, save DCs, and encounter complexity increase.

fighter-subclass-feature-15.summary: Subclass power spikes again with a specialized veteran-tier benefit.
fighter-subclass-feature-15.description: Treat this as a meaningful tactical upgrade that shifts encounter choices, not a background passive.

fighter-action-surge-2-uses.summary: Gain a second Action Surge use for more frequent tempo-breaking turns.
fighter-action-surge-2-uses.description: Two Action Surge uses let fighters create multiple swing rounds in difficult encounters or long adventuring days.
fighter-action-surge-2-uses.recovery: Regain all expended uses on a Short or Long Rest.
fighter-action-surge-2-uses.effect: Gain one extra action on your turn when activated.

fighter-ability-score-improvement-16.summary: Penultimate ASI/feat to lock in your final form before capstone play.
fighter-ability-score-improvement-16.description: Level 16 is typically used to complete core optimization or pick a final feat that rounds out mobility, control, or defense.

fighter-indomitable-3-uses.summary: Indomitable reaches three uses, greatly reducing the risk of failed clutch saves.
fighter-indomitable-3-uses.description: High-level encounters throw repeated save pressure; this upgrade helps fighters keep acting through control and burst effects.
fighter-indomitable-3-uses.recovery: Regain all expended uses on a Long Rest.

fighter-subclass-feature-18.summary: Your archetype unlocks a near-capstone technique at level 18.
fighter-subclass-feature-18.description: This late subclass milestone should read as a practical headline benefit that changes how turns are sequenced.

fighter-extra-attack-4.summary: Your Attack action now makes four attacks each turn.
fighter-extra-attack-4.description: At level 20, four attacks define the fighter's top-end sustained weapon pressure and synergy with mastery effects.

fighter-subclass-capstone.summary: Your martial archetype culminates with its defining capstone payoff.
fighter-subclass-capstone.description: The subclass capstone is the final expression of your archetype identity and should be surfaced as a marquee power.

fighter-epic-boon.summary: Gain an Epic Boon that grants a mythic-tier benefit beyond normal class scaling.
fighter-epic-boon.description: Epic Boons represent endgame customization and should be tracked as a major reward choice with clear impact text.

fighter-weapon-mastery-5-weapons.summary: Expand mastery to five weapons for maximum tactical coverage.
fighter-weapon-mastery-5-weapons.description: At top tier, a fifth mastery slot lets fighters tailor weapon choice to encounter geometry, resistances, and control priorities without losing identity.

progressionSummary.summary: Fighting Style, Second Wind, Weapon Mastery.
Ability Score Improvement, Weapon Mastery (5 weapons).

buildTips.primaryReason: powers your weapon attacks and Athletics checks
buildTips.secondaryReason: increases your HP and Concentration saving throws
buildTips.avoidDumpReason: rarely used by Fighters, safe to set to 8-10
